entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: The legume crop under study for pest resistance.

entities.name: HELICOVERPA ARMIGERA
entities.description: The gram pod borer, a major pest of chickpea.

entities.name: ACID EXUDATES
entities.description: Organic acids secreted by chickpea plants associated with resistance to pests.

entities.name: BACILLUS THURINGIENSIS
entities.description: A bacterium used as a biological control agent against H. armigera.

entities.name: RESISTANCE TO POD BORER
entities.description: The ability of chickpea genotypes to resist infestation by H. armigera.

entities.name: FOLIAR SPRAY
entities.description: A method of applying Bacillus thuringiensis to plants.

entities.name: TRANSGENIC PLANTS
entities.description: Plants genetically modified to express Bacillus thuringiensis proteins.

entities.name: LEAF FEEDING
entities.description: The amount of leaf tissue consumed by larvae.

entities.name: LARVAL SURVIVAL
entities.description: The rate at which larvae survive on a host plant.

entities.name: LARVAL WEIGHT
entities.description: The physical weight of the larvae during development.

entities.name: ICC 506EB
entities.description: A resistant chickpea genotype used as a check.

entities.name: C 235
entities.description: A chickpea genotype used in the study.

entities.name: ICCV 10
entities.description: A chickpea genotype used in the study.

entities.name: L 550
entities.description: A chickpea genotype used in the study.

entities.name: ANTIFEEDANT EFFECTS
entities.description: Effects that discourage or reduce the consumption of food by the pest.

entities.name: BIOLOGICAL ACTIVITY
entities.description: The effectiveness of Bacillus thuringiensis in controlling the pest.

relationships.description: Associated with resistance to pod borer
Reduces the biological activity of Bacillus thuringiensis
Possesses high levels of acid exudates
